title: My most popular posts of 2021

content:
2021 was the year I got serious about blogging.

Thanks to friends for the encouragement to do so! I set up this current incarnation of my blog on a software stack I dislike (ruby & jekyll), but I probably won’t change things until it breaks beyond repair. Anyway, I (re)launched the blog filled with my collected writings spanning the past ~20 years.

How many posts?

  • 55 blog posts (up from 42 in 2020)

Most popular posts

According to Google Analytics, my 5 most popular posts in 2021 were (most popular first):

  • Tomoya Ikeda - Macintosh Artist (16 Dec)
  • Turning an iPad Pro into the Ultimate Classic Macintosh (17 Apr)
  • Daily Driver: Channelling RGB into 1-bit (5 Jun)
  • Aquaplus P/ECE (vs Panic Playdate) (19 Aug)
  • Pixel-Perfect retro gaming on LCD 480p EDTVs (6 May)

Looking back

My personal faves from the year 2021 (most recent first):

  • Band-kun バンドくん (1990, KOEI) the one where I discovered a cool old game
  • Electric Brain, et al. the one where I scanned a load of magazines/fanzines
  • The BeOS Bible the one where I scanned an absolutely massive book
  • Daily Driver: Showreel the one where I showed off a game
  • Iwata Asks: Downloader the one where I scraped the web
